message: Node started: Given the Trie structure, calculate the minimum bits to add. For any node that is marked as an end-of-ID, if it has descendants that are also end-of-ID, we must extend the current ID to a leaf. However, the problem asks for the minimum total bits such that no ID is a prefix of another. This is equivalent to ensuring that for every node that is an end-of-ID, it has no descendants that are also end-of-ID. If a node is an end-of-ID and has children, we must push the 'end-of-ID' status down to a leaf or add bits to the existing IDs to make them unique. Specifically, for each node that is an end-of-ID, if it has children, we must add bits to the current ID to make it a leaf that is not a prefix of any other. Actually, the rule is: no cow's reported prefix can be the full ID of another. This means no ID can be a prefix of another. Calculate the minimum bits to add to each string such that no string is a prefix of another, using a greedy approach on the Trie: for each node that is an end-of-ID, if it has children, we must extend it. The cost is the sum of (depth of leaf - depth of original ID) for all cows.
